text:
TLDR: They took money after a free trial and getting it back is a nightmare. 

Signed up to test T-Mobile home internet service with two weeks trial period. Service seemed fine for what it was, but I got a better price from the service provider I already had, so I canceled with T-Mobile. Store couldn't cancel service, so I had to call. Customer service has been kind every time, but they are useless. I was charged for a month of service ($55) after I had canceled on time and returned all equipment. Upon calling for reimbursement I received $40.21, not the full repayment. That was two weeks ago and the process is on-going. I'm still owed the small amount and it's barely worth the repeated calls and confusion from service reps. I'm no legal expert, but if this is happening on a large scale it seems criminal to me. Promised free trial and they took the money anyway, making it painful to get full refund. If this is happening as a result of their "system" it looks like fraud to me, taking money from people with already tight budgets who need cheap internet when they promise a free trial. Won't use them again.

T mobile lies to its customers- sells u one deal at one rate & when ur bill comes it's substantially higher. This has happened once to me & once to my mother-in-law. How they get away w this is disgusting. They make u think it's ur ineptitude in understanding the bill
DISGUSTING....no it's not & hope they get sued over & over. Disgusting ppl running the company. May they rot!!!!

I have insurance on screen protector...went to store on davis...no one would wait on me...helped people who came in after me... was there for over 1.5 hourdcalled another store...no one would answer...called 611...they can't help me since I'm on my moms plan...and she wasn't at my house to talk to agent...and I thought vetizon was bad...guess I will have to go back to them...this is unacceptable

T Mobile is overhyped and overrated.   I switched from Verizon due to cost & customer service. T Mobile customer service is second to none.  That's where it ends.  The T Mobile service doesn't live up to its hype.  I drop calls like itthe 80's and service is none existent in many stores or buildings. When my 2 years are up, I will return to Verizon.

Don’t go to the Miller Lane (Butler Township Ohio) store. They are thief’s. They stole my two phones I turned in and now am getting charged 2000. I have the boxes and serial numbers and now must contact the Ohio Attorney General today after they said the employees working there were all fired. They’ve been running us around for several months with excuses and say they are fixing but never do anything. They will be getting a call this week from a Consumer Complaint Specialist from the Attorney General’s office.

They suck. Updated to T Life so customers can't access T Mobile Tuesday's offers & suck us into buying new expensive phones to bow down to them.  Bad enough they don't take auto bill on credit cards anymore. They're making BILLIONS & screwing customers again.  And the service is crap.

very bad exprience with T-mobile today..After being with t-mobile for over 15 years customer service was no help at all..I have always paid for insurance for my Samsung Galaxy phone warranty and since Assurance didnt have it in stock they made me take a very inferior phone called WingTech which gets horrible reviews…Regardless that i pay for a warrenty for Samsung they force you to take it or leave it…I will be looking into other carriers now and leaving T-mobile as soon as possible

As a long-standing T-Mobile customer subscribed to the Magenta® Amplified plan with three voice lines, my experience with the company has been consistently positive without any notable issues. However, on January 15, 2024, we initiated contact with T-Mobile to help us navigate plans and get the best deal possible at that time.  To stay on the current plan and purchase all new phones and increase our monthly bill from $ 157 to $270 or switch to a more expensive plan and get promotions. We opted to switch to the Go5G Next plan, which involved trading in three devices: upgrading from an iPhone 12 to an iPhone 15 Pro and obtaining one complimentary iPhone 15 due to adding a line. During this process, 2 different T-Mobile representatives assured us that, after factoring in all eligible discounts, including a teacher discount, our monthly bill would total $230. The process included 3 steps including changing the plan which is only possible by phone service. We trusted representatives to do it correctly, but unfortunately, it was not the case. The wrong code was applied to one of the lines, the other code was expired and had no value.  
Despite receiving three new iPhone 15 Pros and one iPhone 15 on January 17, our account still reflected details from the previous Magenta plan. It was only last week that T-Mobile finally updated our account to the Go5G Next plan, resulting in a monthly bill exceeding $349. It feels that we are trapped because according to the agreement we only have 20 days to make changes, but our account information was not updated till it was too late. Until that time (February 6) we were sure that our bill would be $230, not $349 like it is stated now.
Upon noticing this discrepancy, I promptly contacted T-Mobile to seek a resolution. Despite investing significant time on the phone with various representatives, the issue with our bill persisted unresolved. I hate to be trapped: phones we used for trade-in cannot be returned to us, and codes for the trade-in are incorrect. The customer service department wasted hours on “investigation’ to come up with “Unfortunately we cannot fix the problem as all those deals are expired now.
They have lied to me about my bill and credit.  They want to charge me 60 over quoted rates. All I hear is how no one can do anything g and they will charge me an early termination.

2 years in a row we signed up for monthly internet service during a lengthy stay over holidays. No problem the first year. The second year they continued to charge us and when I called to straighten it out was told only the account holder can cancel. This was not the case the previous year, nor were we told this when we signed up and took the equipment or when we returned it. And it is nowhere in their paperwork. Customer service just says, "I'm sorry" or "I understand" or blah, blah, blah. I'm pretty sure the supervisor is just the robot at the next desk. So literally, any authorized user can add lines and services because that makes T-mobile money. But when it comes to canceling services and therefore reducing the monthly intake on their end, it takes the account holder. Unethical much? And when I complained that my son (not an authorized user) was able to walk into a T-Mobile store and get a brand new I-Phone, she suggested I go to the store (where he was attending college?!! ) and take it up with the manager. Again with the customer service script that says, "That shouldn't have happened." Forget reading small print, it isn't there.

Left T-Mobile due to poor service and dead spots.  Went to local store where they told me I was paid in full.  Received a bill a week later AND a call from a collection company.  Fought this collection. Weeks later I receive a FINAL bill from T-Mobile.  I pay it.  Today I receive a REVISED FINAL bill for $107.00, finally with an explanation of charges. This is four months after I was told I was paid in full. Worst.

I was here two times, associates do not like use time to help customer problems, just they want to sale with out answer questions!! I purchased my I phone by calling t mobile and they were very friendly. I came back to south Pasadena store and asked them help me to transfer my old i phone information to new i phone one of the associate said we can not. I went to Duarte store and they did for me very friendly.

The actual speed is too low.
We buy this Net service from t-mobile since it offers $10.00 off (the full NET service total becomes $40.00)
1. The average speed measured is usually less than 25 Mbit (less than a quarter of the lowest standard 100Mbit/s speed). 
2. Customer Tech Support Never has a real solution. All solution available is to turn off the power of the Router box. Within 8-days, we made at least 4 calls, but the only real solution is "to turn it off about 5 minutes then turn it on again".
3. Lie in explaining reason: within 4 calls we reach, the only explanation is "I saw the technical is working  ... It only takes 1-2 days". But actually, it is either a lie or they work with the turtle speed. After many days the problem was still not resolved.

T-Mobile is refusing to honor their Phone insurance policy with me. Instead they want me to buy a new phone. This is INSURANCE AND CONSUMER FRAUD
They want me to pay $1400 for a new phone when I pay $18 a month to cover my phone. THIS IS THE BIGGEST SCAM EVER.. What a joke they are as a company. I really think they are doing this because I am a senior

Ameen Tawoosi has provided wonderful and patient help on multiple occasions.
Store #5017 
Lincolnwood Town Center

I purchased international data plan at $30 for two phones to travel for ten days and was unable to use any of the 5 gbs on one phone. Whenever I tried to access internet it wouldn’t let me and times out.  I called T mobile and went through automated service only for it to say to call back during business hours. I called when I got back to get refund and they treated me like I was a liar even though the phone showed that 0 out of 5gbs were used. I tried reaching out to managers and they were even worse. I escalated to another manager above two and that person never called back. I have been loyal and never asked for anything from this company but when you sell me a product that doesn’t work and then treat me like dirt for $30 when I pay $230+ per month it is unforgivable. They wanted to refund me half saying it was my fault that I didn’t constantly reach out to them while overseas and figure out when they were open to help me. Huh! They said that the phone was used because it stated for one day out of ten 0.004 kb were used and that the phone was used on the day I left and was still in the U.S. Despicable! Others have complained about bad service and I will make sure that friends who are chief officers at major companies spread the word. And you will lose a bunch of customers for $15!

I wish I would have read these reviews before switching to this $#!t show company. I had to pay $800 to sign up.  I went from Verizon and having internet at work most of the night to this joke of a place having internet 1 hour maybe a night.  Hopefully it's not a run around to get my money back.  2 days of this bs company is more than enough for me. I spoke to customer service 4 times in the last 2 days.  They were also less than useless because none of them speak English.  They mute the phone over and over and try saying the same stupid thing that don't work.  This company is a joke!!! I would rather have no phone than have this trash company for free!

I've been with T Mobile for YEARS! I wasn't even able to do a simple stupid trade in without having to pay money monthly because I was "grandfathered in on an old plan". 

TMobile?? GET A FREAKIN' GRIP! 

You heathens just LOST my service!

I mean they've got the most STUPIDEST standards, rules, and stipulations I've ever heard of!

I can get the EXACT phone from a different. Carrier for FREE, but only pay the service!

Thank goodness for other competitors!

I had a business account with T-Mobile, with 10 phone lines and a couple of other devices. They guaranteed me credits of $600 per line to switch over to them. Once I switched, they said that I didn’t qualify for those credits and only got $150 per line. My first invoice was incorrectly $1500. In calling them, they said that was a mistake, and I should only pay what I owed, which was about $500. I did that, but two weeks later, they shut off my lines and charge me to turn them back on. They did this four subsequent times over three months. I finally had to transfer out. They are now invoicing me for over $1000 for a phone they say I purchased from them that I had originally bought from Verizon and ported in when I first transferred to them. This company is an absolute train wreck, I have spent countless hours working on it to get resolved. I even come lost a few clients because they could not reach me. They keep telling me they will have someone call me back to me, but they never do. They have now sent my bill for a phone that I owned before I came to them, to collections. Absolutely corrupt!

This company would not be getting any referral regarding g new customers from me. I have been with this company for 5 years plus and customer service sucks. This company would getva -10 rating. I advice no one to get your services with them. They have f...Ed up my account many times.

Went into another T-mobile kios.
What a clown show. I only wanted a camera case. Wait, wait, wait. Five employees and two customers. What a mess. I have enter T-Mobile kios location several times just for a small purchase. Everytime I wait, wait, wait and finally leave. Nine times now and still nothing. I finally moved on. I didn't want to leave but enough is enough. JOKE!

These people suck I got the home internet and a phone for a 15-day tryout. The Samsung A 15 phone is broken and I have asked them twice for proof of purchase and a return shipping label they will not and have not sent me either. I have filed a complaint against them with the FCC about this crap with them they suck.
If I could leave 0 stars, I would. I was a Nextel customer then Sprint, now T-Ghetto. T-mobile has thee worst, unprofessional and rude agents. Nextel and Sprint was far better...

This place is a joke, I purchased a new device and the 1st week it stopped making and receiving calls, text etc., just a phone brick. T mobile said I will need to buy a new phone due to i am past the 15-day buyer's remorse period. So now I am paying for service on a device that I can't use at all. They said they need to send me the same device even though it's documented a bad device from all reviews. I will never use T Mobile service again in my lifetime, I will gladly pay more at ATT or Verizon to get the service I pay for.

Mada a huge mistake moving from ATT. From day 1 not happy with how my porting started and the false promotion they promised. 

My honest recommendation to AT T users please do not switch from ATT, if your are switching please don't come to TMobile because of the below reasons.

1. Poor network coverage. They say we support wifi calling , when you are at work you don't have a wifi network and my office is in NYC and I have only 1 point signal. I never had this issue with ATT 

2. promotions what ever they have won't continue even for a week. The promotions whatever they offered me during the sales got expired before I activated all my numbers. I had to wait couple of days to get my phone unlocked from  ATT by then they said your promotions is expired and I am no more eligible for the promotional offer signed up. More over the promotions is eligible only if we activate all number in your account I order to get all lines. 

3. We are 5 in the plan the promotions said free phones on all lines when we added free phones we neee asked to pay  800 for the 5th and said this gets credited month on month. Which was not explained by the sales person in the store. We have 5 lines what will the other members think about me when I say this offer no more exists. I am adding them to my line by saying the promotional benefits. What kind trust will I have.my name gets spoiled because of this 

4. Porting and activation my line was not activated for 24 hrs after porting happened. I did not realize the phone wasn't activated until next day. I had to call them to activate Mt number wherein I had to wait 40 mins on call to get connected 

Overall I'm with wrong hands and no more I will refer or recommend my friends and family to T mobile.  After 2 yrs I will switch back to ATT.

I have had the service for a year and 4 months and it the service center that is in the Philippines is the most atrocious comments said to customers I have ever heard and if you complain you they put you on hold,  Comments that have been said to me include, go get a cup of coffee and get a cookie, to just begin with.  They have been charging me for tablets that I do not have every month and I have to be on the phone for 1-2 hours every billing cycle.  I know their prices are good but the customer service is enough to make you insane.

Review of T Mobile
I ordered T Mobile Wireless Internet. I never received the proper equipment for internet and was charged $112 for a service i did not receive. I returned something i did receive to the store, and they told me it was not the proper equipment for internet. I asked for a refund and they said i had to call a number to do it. I asked can't they do it there? They said no. I called the number and said i didn't receive the proper equipment. They said that means i can get a refund. Then they quizzed me and asked for a 6 digit pin #. I said i don't remember any pin #. Nobody can remember a six digit pin #. They said i had to go to the store to get the refund. I said I was just at the store and they said to call this number. They said that is the only way. I went to the website and tried to log in and it didn't recognize me. Then i went to the store and explained the situation fully. The store clerk asked if i wanted to pay the amount. I said no, i want it cleared out, i was told that they could do that here. She said that she would not service me and asked me to leave! 
this was 4714 N Broadway, Chicago IL 60640 at 1pm Sunday Feb 4 2024
I said i would file a dispute with the credit bureau and this is what i intend to do. I am sticking with Comcast, even though they charge more they don't play these funny games with customers wasting people's time to cheat them.

Worst unhelpful customer service I've ever dealt with. They charged my card for 90 bucks when I never activated service. They don't know where my account is depending on who I talk to, sometimes they find it, some say they will refund my money but never do. This has been going on for months. I recently got home internet and they don't even know how to apply my acp benefit which I'm approved for and transfer me to other companies that have absolutely nothing to do with anything. Their website says they accept the acp benefit and I follow the steps to apply it on my online account yet it just logs me out and I cant do anything. It appears they only accept the acp benefit with metro and assurance wireless.
They’re overcharging me and giving me horrible excuses for the extreme amount of money I’m having to pay.

Stay away! I had tmobile for almost 10 years. At first they were good, over time the service (phone) has been awful for me everywhere. I even had to call multiple times to see what was going on. Was super patient, even tried out their internet. Which sucked in my area and I had to cancel it within 4 months. Anyways the service never improved so I finally left them! And ever since they’ve been treating me bad, I’ve called to pay off my remaining bill and no one helps me. Tried the chat same thing. I received a bill by mail that told me to pay online as a guest and their system won’t let me either. Overall bad phone and internet service and unfortunately bad customer service!! which is why they lost a great client with me

Come to find out that they charged my card without my permission for the remaining balance on the phone and the $1 bill owed them for service. Horrible company! I’m appalled, this is illegal

First off, was lied to about a promo deal. The guy who opened our account in the store said the promo came with 2 phones, 2 tablets and a galaxy watch absolutely free. Told him we didnt want the tablets or watch and he said the price  was barely lower without so we should take the promo.  He drew out a plan and said our total amount was $206 including taxes and that would never increase. Realized when we got home that we were charged to activate the tablets and watch. We knew we were paying phone activation fees but were never explained to that we had to pay activation fees for the 3 items we didnt even want to begin with. Tried to take the tablets and watch back the next day and same guy was there, along with the manager who was very unfriendly and unapologetic, and was told we can return them but need to pay a restocking fee and also our promo deal would not be valid anymore and the price would go up so it was in our best interest to keep the devices we didnt want. So we did. Few days later, the bill on my account was $251 so we called customer service.  She didnt know why it was that amount.  Then my husband put in for the first responder discount and auto pay discount and she said the bill would lower to $135 within 1 to 3 days. It did not. Called CS again and the girl said the first responder discount went through and now our bill would be $224 monthly. Asked to talk to manager because the price was higher than we were quoted...and come to find out , phones werent free. We have to pay monthly for them. And the tablets and watch services are also  not included in that "promo" price we were quoted. He said we had to go back to the store if  we wanted to return the devices and would be charged those restock fees but we would qualify for a better discount. So all in all, we will have paid close to $200 in activation and restocking fees for 3 devices we clearly told the 1st guy we didnt want. We will have nothing to show for it but wasted time, aggravation,  and $200 wasted as well. Once our contract is up, we will be leaving tmobile for good and i would NEVER recommend anyone go there, especially the lanoka harbor nj store. No one is held accountable for giving us false info, or straight out lies even. Talked to 3 reps on the phone, one a manager, and 3 reps in store, also including one manager. Every representative had a different answer to give and gives you the run around on who to talk to so you go in circles calling CS and going back to the store. They are a disgusting company with incompetant liars for employees!

Edit: just went to the corporate store in toms river nj and the employees and manager were very helpful, and apologetic for what happened in the other store. They got us on the plan we originally wanted and did their best to refund what they could. I recommend going to that store if any one ever wants to deal with t mobile

honestly criminal, I got a phone at the store which had many issues that they replaced. however, they didnt have the replacement in stock, they ordered one. It didnt show for two weeks and i couldnt continue to have a phone that worked 50% of the time. I went back in and the store gave me the next best one for no upcharge. HERE IS WHERE THINGS WENT WRONG. new person, did the activation under a "Lease" even though i paid for the phone in full originally. They were not able to back up the error because the IMEI and other numbers were already in the system because of the activation. After HOURS they gave me a credit for the "lease" on my account. fast forward until now, lease is up and they are asking me if i want to KEEP my phone or RETURN it. well its mine, i paid in full. i called in to tell them this and they argued, told me i was wrong, they said i got the upgraded phone and its worth more so NOW i need to pay for it. total joke, scam, and should be illegal.
Trash and unprofessional never getting no money from me

I've been T-Mobile for about 2 years. I had an interest in upgrading my phone so, I did my homework, decided on a phone and began the omupgrade on the T-Mobile website 
 The figures provided were fine however, when you get to the consent page (to agree to the figures) everything changes to higher monthly payment amounts, your trade-in value is nowhere to be found etc.....
I previously thought T-Mobile was a halfway decent carrier but, they're nothing but corrupt, unethical scumbags.
Some Pinhead rep told me their "system" wasn't functioning properly and he then quoted me the price I originally saw. Too late! They lost my trust. Time to move on to another carrier.....

After 3 wonderful years I just had the most disappointing conversations with T-Mobile. What's changed??  My services were suspended with no advance notice, with no messages, no communication whatsoever. The reason, I'm told, is because there was one missing payment. Meantime T-Mobile has not 1 but 2 valid forms of payment and I have never missed any previous payments. After spending crazy time on the phone trying to understand and rectify the issue I was then told different answers by different supervisors. The last crazy conversation was about a $15 credit I should have received and was not provided. Enough is enough. Good by T-mobile until you return what you used to be.

I have t mobile internet and at 1st it was great. Then summer time came and internet was off and on off and on. They gave me credits. The internet is always iffy, now In January I find myself watching old movies and fights instead of Netflix or curiosity stream because my internet is off and on off and on. I'm tired of paying for half ass internet.  Its either At@t or t mobile.  T mobiles customer service is 100 times better. Just wish their internet was as good. It sucks paying for streaming services when you can't watch them. I'm tired of circles spinning on the TV and my cell phone saying no internet connection

edits.text: I have been stuck in At@t hell for my internet service. I switched to t mobile and I'm so happy with the service. Chelsea set up the new modem, when I came home I plugged in the power and it was already set up and working great. So happy with my new set up. Thank you

text: Worst customer service! They had a problem with our internet, it took from 1/9/24 until today 1/26/24 to finally get service. It took a change of a modem and we were supposed to make another trip to get a second modem today but there is a snowstorm so we didn’t go. During the last hour, service magically came back on. We spoke to them as we have many, many times this month and they would not give us a refund on this month’s bill. So instead of 300 gb, we were only able to use 23 gb, but they still wanted full payment! After my husband spoke with them, they gave us $40 off our bill and told us it was the best their manager could allow because they didn’t know if we would use a thousand gb tonight! So $93.57 for this month of 23gb. Terrible customer service! We are looking to switch.

Worst ever, employees do a ripoff not explaining none of their offers or details of their packages. Got a prepaid card, payed 3 months in advance to keep the line active, cause I’ll be out of the US (no one told me I won’t be able to receive SMS nor calls and the line will be dead), so those 3 months in advance payment is wasted as the line is inactive but payed for. When I called them, they told me to upgrade to 40$ plan to just be able to receive SMS to the line, and calls if any. I told them I just need the line active, and I payed in advance cause T-Mobile guess what - receiving payments from cards issued only from certain countries and my home country isn’t the one. I told them, I’m outside of the US, payed in advance to have the line active - none informed me of additional minimum charge for prepaid roaming, and I can’t upgrade my plan as the card I have isn’t from one of 15 countries which is accepted. Money wasted time wasted stress from dealing with thiefs. Zero zero zero stars, do not get anything from this ripoff and their uninterested staff - worst experience in Seattle and Chicago! Philippinos on the call center are friendly, however talking much and zero helpful as their company is - zero.

Was contacted by a T mobile business rep. Said they could lower our bill. Instead they open 3 accounts and triple billed us. Called customer service repeatedly but tge barely English speaking reps couldn’t help because there business rep. opened accounts. The internet barely worked. Horrible company. I left for Verizon and never got our money back.

I have been with tmoble for ever. I just bought a new phone, and be honest, I wish I had my old one. Not only was it expensive, it is terrible. Talking on phone I hear echo, on speaker, they hear ehcos. Always kicking me off of internet, pictures are terrible. I can't afford a new phone, but seriously thinking of moving on to new company to get one.

The only way to improve this company is to shut it down. They charge for items they say are a gift for loyal customers ( you discover it later on your bill). They steal items you trade in, and then the customer service simply doesn't consider your complaints anymore. They are overpriced. What else? Sometimes you find a competent associate, but it's pure chance.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for over 10 years, but my experience has been far from satisfactory. Living in a densely populated area, I expected reliable cell coverage. However, T-Mobile's service was so inadequate that they had to provide an amplifier to boost the signal. It was one (two, actually) more devices running in our house, but it solved the issue.

Fast forward to when I decided to cancel my service due to moving abroad. To my astonishment, T-Mobile charged me $300 for the amplifier they provided 8 years ago - a piece of equipment that is practically obsolete and that they sold to customers for only $100 back in 2019. 

Attempting to resolve this issue, I spent several hours with their customer service. While the representatives were friendly, it felt like they weren't really listening (I had to repeat some basic facts several times). They never truly addressed my concerns. 

Needless to say that I'll never be a customer again.

T-mobile contacted me about their 5G home internet service. The t-mobile rep offered me one price, but when the bill arrived it was over 50% more. I called and was told that offer had expired, and they would not honor the price. But I never asked for it. It was T-Mobile who made the offer. I am a current t-mobile customer and have been for years. Now how can I trust an offer or anything that I'm told going forward. Too bad!
T-Mobile encouraged me to buy a SIM card they knew wouldn’t work for my phone. Now I’m out 50 dollars and a terrible customer service experience.

My husband and I have used Sprint/TMobile for several years and have had HORRIBLE experiences with them. We’ve tolerated questionable billing practices, bad customer service, inept management, spotty coverage, lousy promotional offers, outright lies about pricing and contract terms, impenetrable website login pages, inestimable wastes of time with general problem resolution, and terrible equipment- related issue resolution. I know mergers are never seamless but this irredeemable mess of a company needs to close down. We can’t wait for our new phone company to take over our account. Run as fast and as far as you can from Sprint/T Mobile if you value your sanity and money.

I have T-Mobile internet and phones They sent me a lap top thru the mail which I told them I did not want numerous times and sent it anyhow. They will not take it back and now they’re charging me for it. They lied about taking it back so now I am stuck with it They told me to sell it . But it only works on t mobile so I am stuck with this useless laptop. I ll be looking for a new service

Worse people to deal with
Managers a d supervisors will not talk to. Customers and no help puts you on hold than transfers you to another useless rep.
I will never recommend any one to them

Absolutely the worst customer service experience I've ever had. Contract was a bait and switch, with false representation of what was included, the "guaranteed" monthly cost, etc. The store said to contact corporate, and corporate said it was the store's responsibility to make things right. Stay away from ALL elements of T-Mobile!

Promises were made by one of their phone "Advisors" and broken as soon as we got of the phone
In my case and my opinion I would say shady business practices.

I have a One Plus 8 phone that I bought from One Plus directly that is locked by T-Mobile. At the time, my carrier used T-Mobile lines so it wasn't a problem. After countless efforts to get T-Mobile to network unlock The phone, I was told that I would need to purchase a prepaid card from them so they could access the phone. Then after having it for about a month and a half, I called again to have it unlocked. Custom Service is clueless! Apparently they don't keep any records of previous phone calls and I have to go through the whole issue again just to have them tell me there's nothing they can do. Finally after at least three supervisor calls, I get one by the name of Alex who convinced me that he had taken care of the situation and my phone would be unlocked in 24-48 hours. He said I would get an email. It's now a week later and my phone is still locked, no email has come, and I am forced to call them again. This time I get Becky. Becky tells me there is no record of my phone calls with Alex or anyone. Funny she did give me a call reference number though. Becky tells me I have to do a factory reset to unlock it. I'm not seeing that this will work either. I will update if it does, but at this point, I have at least 15 hours of talk time to customer service in which ten of those were on hold. I still am not able to use my phone with my regular carrier. Don't ever get T-Mobile! They are terrible! They hire people that you can't understand and it doesn't matter because they don't know what they are doing anyway. I think I may have to complain to the FCC. T-Mobile has no right to keep my phone locked. Thanks for nothing Alex!
Liars, Cheats, and Thieves all

T-mobile is the most deceptive, Greedy, and scammers that I have fully experienced with this company stole money three times from my account without having my permission  I'm not happy with the service and the company which I believe is a crooked establishment

After being with a competitive Telephone Carrier for decades, I decided their fees were too high, and decided to save money by going with T-Mobile and their advertised rates. 

HUGE MISTAKE.

Signed up, and spent THREE (3) days trying to get both of my lines working.  NOTHING worked correctly.  THREE (3) days of wasted time.  They couldn't get both lines connected, service quality was worse than poor, and their agents were totally clueless.   

I then cancelled service, switched to a new Carrier and WHOA  !!!!  everything immediately worked perfectly. BUT !!!   T-Mobile wasn't finished. They had me set up in 'auto-pay' and thus began a multi-month disaster of trying to get them to remove incorrect charges.  HOURS spent on the phone, talking with agents who promised to transfer me to their Supervisor, but who instead switched me back into the general call incoming 'pool'  wherein I had to Start ALL-OVER-AGAIN with a new agent, describing the issues, and trying to find an iota of justice.   More DAYS spent trying to get rid of these bottom feeding losers.  BUT, it's even WORSE !!! They automatically put my account into 'COLLECTIONS and then more clueless scammers calling, trying to collect monies on a NON-EXISTING account.   

STAY AWAY from these low life brain dead liars, and scum.  They cheat, steal, and scam the innocent public.    OTHER carriers are FAR more professional and courteous.  T-Mobile will NEVER achieve even a minimal level of professionalism.  

Please NO DOT blame me when you get 'burned' by these low life scum.

After 6 weeks of frustrating, fruitless calls to T-Mobile customer service I finally walked into the T-Mobile store in person -- after my third visit to the store, a rep was present who had my problem solved in about 15 minutes. I had spent approximately 15 hours over 6 weeks of calling T-Mobile customer service and spoke to so many different reps, supervisors, technical engineers, etc., and none of them could find a solution. Many of them promised to call me back with a solution, and none of them followed through on the promise, and many of them failed to even call me back at all. The DIGITS app was not working dependably--I was continually not receiving all texts. I used DIGITS for my business line and I wanted to keep my business number on my same phone. Why did it take so long for a T-Mobile rep to suggest adding a second line to my phone??? It seems like a simple answer. I wasted a lot of time before finally having my issue solved.

Score one for the girls! Kyla turned my whole situation around. Look break ups suck but when one plays dirty well….. you have to think outside the box. Thanks for all your help and my new upgraded iPhone!

Horrible service at TMobile! I’ve been a loyal customer for 25 years. I get ooor reception on the lines, overcharging of my bills, and poor explanation of service and benefits. This company needs an overhaul!!! No one can answer a strait question! I don’t recommend them!

I need consistent internet for work. I signed up in September and after a week or 2 it worked okay. Then December came and the speed came down 20 times what it was. My co-workers couldn't see or hear me on Teams. 

T-Mobile internet support told me it was because "they can't stop new users from signing up"- that's why it's sooo slow!! OH MY! You must be joking!!

Now I have to get new internet- what a poor customer experience that was! Beware, DON'T TRUST T-MOBLE FOR INTERNET- you may get screwed on speed (the tech said I'd have to wait 6-18 months for another tower to be put in to have any speed!, and YES, LEADERSHIP KNOWS this.)

I am not a customer of T-Mobile but am receiving nuisance calls from their call centre on average 3-4 times per week. I have repeatedly asked them to take my number off the list & stop calling me but yet the issue persists for over a year. My number is registered with tps so who knows how they got it. Awful. I am sick of the stress & interruptions.

1 star because 0 wasn't an option.  T-Mobile raised my rates so I decided to switch to another carrier.  They canceled my service but kept auto-billing me.  I was on the phone with customer service for hours, completely useless and unhelpful.

My husband changed his cell and our home to t mobile. Thank heavens I kept my phone with Verizon. They cut service off before the due date. He is constantly getting his service interrupted. He has made like 5 payments in two months and pays whatever they tell him to pay but our service is interrupted every other week and it's frustrating spending hours on my Verizon cell phone calling T-Mobile to figure out what on earth has happened and if they can get it turned back on. They usually want another payment. I've never seen a company act like this ever. It's insanity. My husband is returning his phone tomorrow and disconnecting his service.  He has promised not to change our home wifi again.

I thought I would save money by using T-Mobile and I did. However, I dropped an average of three calls per day for the ten months I had this service. They could not offer me any better service so I canceled.
Then, after canceling, I was late on my final payment. After they did receive the payment a month late, they billed me late charges and sent me to a collection agency. I received both bills in the same week.
Terrible service and terrible billing procedures. So glad I went back to my old carrier.

I don’t want to sound like I'm exaggerating and writing with a lot of emotions after multiple times trying to get their support. I want to ensure that they have been by far the worst customer service I have experience with all the mobile service companies. If you look forward to have good customer service and have a company that ensures they improve on this, T-Mobile is not the one. Also depending on the area that you live the cell reception is very poor. I recommend to ensure that fits but the customer service is the worst of all companies. Hopefully they improve that soon and don’t keep losing their customers.

I've been with T-Mobile for quite a few years, but sadly, the customer service is severely gone downhill
i’ve done many upgrades in the past with no problems in mailing back the trade-in device
where my problem comes this time I did
A upgrade last November to a very expensive phone got the phone no problem activated
It mailed back my trade-in phone tracked it. It shows received first part of December where the problem lies I had paperwork showing I was supposed to get $585 for that phone I have never received the promised credit to my account I have now had 12 phone calls to T-Mobile telling me to keep waiting they showed the phone has been received, but it may take a while to get the credit last phone call now is telling me to wait two more bill cycles BS!! 
I have done a lot of upgrades. It does not take two months to get your credit. I used to work in a large wireless call center. I know how it works and this is nothing but BS that they are feeding me.
I have called multiple times asking for a manager only to be told one is not available or one would call me back, but they never do
I am severely close to just paying off my phones and taking my lines to another carrier. Only problem is I live in an area where I’m really limited on cell phone carriers as cell reception. Here is not great unfortunately
all I can say is save yourself, major major grief. If you decide to do an upgrade, go to a store, never do one through the mail, or be prepared to eat the money you were promised better yet stay the hell away from
t-Mobile

I responded to a promotion where if I signed up for T-Mobile internet, I would get a 150 dollar virtual Master Card. When I completed the transaction, I tried to use the code given me and it was rejected by the T-Mobile promotions website. I called multiple times and was told by a representative that the offer had expired in November of 2023. Since the promotion was offered to me on December 28th, and the purchase made on December 30th, this is highly unlikely. He then told me that I was only eligible for a 50 dollar reward. 
I am most likely going to cancel and return everything.

What a very sore company I tell them I had covid and that I won't have no money until the 19th and that my account will go negative if they take it out and what do they do still take it out and they said that they would help me but yet they still took it out I will be changing carriers once this is done and over!!!!
Every day worse service and poor signal every day and promotions worse than ever, but the competition is worse there is nowhere to run.

My wife signed up for a T-mobile account for her new iPhone. The account was almost immediately hacked. My wife went to China to care for her mother. Then she discovered her T-mobile account was hacked. The thieves were able to completely take over the phone number. All sms verifications went to the the thieves. They were then able to hack all her bank accounts. They even extended the limit on her credit cards and drained them. We were trying to close the T-mobile account the whole time. T-mobile refused to close the account and every time my wife tried to create a new bank account the thieves used the T-mobile account to take over the new bank account. T-mobile refused all assistance. Now my wife is stuck in China with no way to dispute the charges made in her name or even create a new bank or credit card. T-mobile has allowed my wife to remain powerless in China and will not allow me to close the account either.

THE WORST CELLPHONE COMPANY!!!!
I HATE T-MOBILE!!!!!!!!!!! 
I REPEAT I HATE T-MOBILE!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! 
NEVER AGAIN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
This company suckers people with contracts. I was told by 7 different  representative different prices of my phone. I was sucker by a white female  My phone was stolen and T-mobile insisted on telling me I was under contract. This means I am responsible for a stolen phone that I will never see again. 2 representative/employees; a rude black female with an attitude named Tiss and a white smart mouth male supervisor name Jason that lives in Charleston South Carolina, insisted on threatened me in to pay as if I still had the phone. I had to pay for Price of phone, accessories, data plan, cords, and screen protect and insurance, even though I don't have the iPhone 14. My account had a 360 insurance, in case the phone is lost or stolen. I was charged for a stolen iPhone 14 pro max. This company didn't care that my phone was stolen or tried to give me another phone. They only care about getting money. NO STARS!!!! SEVERAL HANDS DOWN WORST CELL PHONE COMPANY!!!!!!!!!!

My wife and I stopped in to a T-Mobile at 5011 Meadowood Mall in Reno and had the best experience ever. Naomi B. was are T-Mobile expert whom assisted us; she answered all are questions and helped through several issues we were having. Thanks Naomi! -Keith and Rebeca
I tranfer to cricket and they continue  charging me for my line. This company is worse that Verizon.

Do not trust t moble at all. I was lied to auburn store about what I would pay, then asked about adding two more lines on phone with some one and lied to again. They tell u a price to get u to sign up. Guarantee that price and then jack the price way up instantly. More people need to step up and turn them in to bbb. They do not have a right to sell u a deal so u switch to find out deal don’t exist.

I have been trying to deal with adding lines as well as a change in ownership of 3 current lines- been a customer for 7 years- the customer service is HORRIFIC.  Sure, they are polite and read their scripted cues beautifully, but they have ZERO idea what they're actually doing and I've gotten incorrect information over and over and over again..... I've spent 30+ hours on hold, talking with a rep, or chatting online to try and get things resolved.  And in this neverending process was just told yesterday that someone by a different name has an account in T-mobile with MY SS#!!!

To start with, if I could give a negative number I would. Today 1/1/2024 my son was trying to switch carriers as he was on our account. When he received the SIM card, we found out his phone was locked, because we owed a $1.32. This was an unknown balance to my husband and I. The $1.32 was sent to collections. My husband and I cancelled our service on 12/5/2023. We switched to PureTalk. My phone ended up being locked as well. My husband took my phone into a T-MOBILE store and was unlocked with no mention of a balance owed and in collections this was on 12/7/2023. We've been on off the phone with T-Mobile Customer Care Services off and on since 4pm (1/1/2024) We have spoken to 
2 different operators on currently on hold for a third who is Supervisor #2...We were previously told that since we cancelled our service before we had a credit of $92.00, again no mention of $1.32 that was owed. Mind you our bill was due by the 9th of each month, so technically our bill was not due until 1/9/2024, if would have stayed with them.  Since, we cancelled 12/5/2023 there should not be bill owed...now it's in collections not even a month later...Way to T-Mobile, you rock and still on the phone at 11:33 at note with no resolution. My husband is going to the Cooperate office tomorrow...

I was a T Mobile customer for nearly 10 years.  Over that period, their customer service has gone downhill.  Hold times to reach customer service are now 30 minutes to an hour.  

On December 26, 2023 at 12:31pm, I called T Mobile to protest an increase due to their declining my autopay discount; they wanted banking account & routing info, which I was not willing to provide.  I was on hold for over 30 minutes.  They subjected the on-hold caller to music and a recorded voice cut in every minute, saying "we'll be right with you" and sometimes asked you to "name that tune".  During the long wait and bot cut-ins, I wondered "why are we being treating so poorly!?"

I decided I'd had enough and cancelled my T Mobile service on 12/26/23.  It turned out to be the worst customer experience I have ever had.  Our two Iphones had a T Mobile SIM/Carrier lock.  I could not begin service with the new carrier until the phones were unlocked. I spoke with a T Mobile tech on 12/26/23 and requested the SIM unlock for the two devices. 

The next day, 12/27/23, the phones remained locked, so I drove to my local T Mobile store, and the employees were unable to help.  They said I had to work with T Mobile's Customer Care group (via phone).

It ultimately took almost 25 hours and about 3.5 hours on the phone with various T Mobile people (plus two more charges on my credit card) for T Mobile to finally unlock the phones.  It was a version of “kick the can”:  one rep told me I needed to do a factory reset on my phone to unlock it.  She transferred me to an Apple Care tech, who advised me that T Mobile needed to unlock the phone and that a factory reset would NOT unlock it.   

In the last call, I was on the phone with a T Mobile tech for well over two hours to get the phones unlocked.    Before I could get the phones unlocked, they asked me about returning equipment I had already returned (a signal booster which didn't work, and that they failed to support, I had returned it months earlier) and about paying them for an Iphone we had paid for in cash months earlier.  

This was just an awful experience.

Worst company I have ever done business with. I switched from T-Mobile to Verizon, they immediately locked me out of my account. It took me three months to get this problem remedied and $288 later. I would advise anyone to stay away from this phone company?!!!

My experience with T Mobile the worst company that I have dealt with in my 76 years. I went.to the Mechanicsville Va store and got the internet gateway to try for 15 days
I could not get a good signal to far from tower. I took it back 4 days later and was told we won't take it back and to drive another 30 minutes to another store where they took it back. That's only the beginning I started getting charged 55.00
A month for 2 months on credit card.I called and they said they would take care of it. I called again two weeks later and they said it would be taken care of
I called again and sorry.it would be taken care of and today I get a collections notice for 110.00
I called again and asked to speak to manage and got no satisfaction. I just filed a complaint with the Attorney General Office ,BBB and FCC to have this removed from my credit report. I will never go near a t Mobile office again
Ps I received a bill for 95.33 for equipment. I received a email from Marty ceo of the response team telling I returned the equipment on 11/16/23 and not on 10/16/23 and he would offer to erase the 95.33 if The claim was over. I sent the receipt for the return showing 10/16  now that's  the ceo of the response team can't even get it right. I made him a offer to have bills showing zero balance and a letter from the so called non collections company alliance saying zero balance is owed.
Sent from my Galaxy

Terrible they will lie to you to go to them and will start charging you for all kinds of different things. They are all trained to get you.had a terrible experience with them I left them and went to AT&T and never looked back.

I have been with t mobile for over 6 years now and slowly they are showing their true face. For the past 3 months i have been really patient. I wanted to upgrade my paid off iphone to their latest version and they sent me the wrong phone and had to send me a new order. Now they have messed up my account and I owe double what I used to. My new monthly bill now is $750 which is ridiculous. I have been on the phone with them every other day and they keep promising we will get to the bottom of this but they never do. They have now suspended my line for the 4th time because I was promised a call and resolution from some of their supervisors who completely ghosted me as this seems to be a more complicated situation. I rely on t mobile for my business and they have not held their end.

It's been great until lately.  I have an issue with the trade-in value that I got for a phone that I had that worked perfectly fine.  I just wanted to upgrade.  
The tech sent me back an email informing me that I would only get a 10% discount because of a display issue.  That meant that there was something wrong with the display on the phone.  There was nothing wrong with the display when I sent it.  I wanted to SEE what the problem was. When I called the agent, I was told that they didn't have that information.  That agent said that he understood my situation and could give me a 20% discount because I was such a good customer and had been and was a long-time customer.  The next billing period the discount that the agent promised was there.  Then the month after, it was gone, so I again called and that agent again understood the issue and he promised the 20%discount.  Again the next billing period the discount was there but only for a month.    The following month the discount was gone completely.  I called this time and the agent was completely rude but he did talk to the supervisor and said that they could put on the original discount but then said smugly, you should feel blessed that they put on the discount because usually when the tech said something is wrong, they don't give any discounts.  I said, Wow,REALLY?!  He also said that the 2 prior agents do not have permission in giving the 20% discount. So that was 2 separate agents that applied the discounts but shouldn't have?   I just wanted them to show me proof of the display issue.  That maybe the Tech made a mistake.    So I am really thinking about changing  Carriers because this was a total headache.

I was with Sprint for a long time, they switched to TMobile. While I was with Sprint I fully paid off every phone I had and always did the trade in to upgrade. If I would have known they would take the phone, and the offer they gave me, when life changed and I switched companies to something cheaper I would have never traded in my phones. I would have just sold them on my own. I lost $400, please learn from my mistake.

They suck. Long phone waits.overseas operators who can’t speak clear English. Rude representatives

I am on hold now they did not tell me that for the first 10 minutes. They don’t care about you. Just the end of the day

edits.text:
I have been a customer of T-Mobile for either eight or nine years and I have always been a good customer. I have always bought more than one phone. I’ve spent a lot of money with T-Mobile over the years.  Well recently I became ill and I’ve been put on long term disability. This particular month my disability check was held up due to a problem and I didn’t get it in time so I called them and made a payment arrangement hoping that my check might come. I was trying to sell something to make the money but that didn’t happen. I called on the date of the payment per the arrangement I spoke to a representative who was nice but her supervisor said no. it was basically “oh well too bad it doesn’t matter if you’re sick”. People  need to be able to contact your doctors.  all that matters to them is that you give them your money. Heaven forbid life gets in the way. So this is gonna make me start looking at other carriers.  I will also make me tell all who use T-Mobile that if they do run into issues one way or another, they can’t trust T-Mobile to be behind you.
Although this to make me look at my bill, and I got a notice two months ago that I had finish paying off an iPhone 12 but I’m still getting charged $10 a month for something I don’t know what it is but they’re charging me for the phone not services size make a phone call on top of that.

text: I used to get good service from T-Mobile, but now it's terrible and I plan to switch companies. I hope I can find a better company. They make mistakes so then I have to call them and wait on hold for half an hour, only to get bad service from the person who just doesn't know how to do their job on the other end who's not from here, and apparently doesn't have the skills to handle customer service.

I don't see any use for the T-mobile store in the Westfield Oakridge Mall. The manager of the store - Eduardo has no powers and they don't honor anything that the T-mobile app online offers. All the sales folks have parroted 'only eligible if you upgrade your plans to 5gGoplus plan'. There was a time when they'd invite and go the distance to retain customer satisfaction but now with their domination in the market, all of them display 'arrogance' - the manager in particular. Is there a way to notify T-mobile upper management to reconsider either removing the manager or empowering the stores to honor online offer or remove the store completely?

I chose the 55+ plan since it allowed two lines, one for me and one for my out-of-town college kid.  I was using a Droid and moved over to the iPhone 11.  Then T-Mobile “upgraded” the antenna my phone was using to connect to.  Weeks of trouble started and when CS ran a “diagnostic” on my connection, it blew out my Cisco WiFi router.  They gave me “generous” $75 to replace it.  No way!  Cisco didn’t sell $100 devices then.  I was told by CSRs that management was considering replacing my phone with a 12.  Right, in a pig’s eye!  I had to buy it and the improvement was minimal.
Calls to customer service went to through to nauseatingly polite representatives, or once in a while people who really didn’t want to work. The big red check is a no go from the start and all the smaller guys are subcontracted to the “big boys” in the market place and offer contracts of dubious, IMHO, value to the customer.

So, as soon as junior finishes his studies, I will return to a flip-phone or no mobile phone.

Experience, what you get immediately AFTER you need it.

Woefully incompetent  tech support mostly from India or China.  Can't understand barely a word they say.  Staff, while friendly are terribly incompetent.  Shipping of new simm card was aborted due to their error.  Advised me to go to local store for new simm.  Local store was better but didn't know how to transfer old number to new account.  I was told to call back their incompetent, online tech support which was just as clueless.  T-Mobile cannot afford competent staff.

Call the 611 because my account was frozen.  Phone tech could not get into my billing. Made payment . Had payment plan $64 on the 21st and $64 on the first of January. Well T-Mobile locked it down because I haven't made the January 1 2024 payment....it's December 21st ....so i had to pay the Jan 1st bill on the 21st just to be able to get my other phone activated. 
The attitude of customer service was good at first till I stood my ground so I put it on speaker phone. Every one in the electronics section heard her crappy attitude asking me "why am I still waiting on you" ...get rid of horrible bs loopholes and sell the company to Temu!!!!!!¡
I spoke with Javon who was knowledgeable and patient. He fixed my problem while being pleasant and easy to work with. I am not a techy.

There is a reason why they don't ever document any conversations or the trade in value of the phone you just gave them.  I was told $1000 for the trade in value, then I sent in my phone, I never got the $1000, I got partial credit like $440, I called them a dozen times about this, each time I was told different BS or excuses, 6 months later I am still calling about the correct trade in value, this time they said my original promotion had expired at the time I sent in my phone.  I've had enough with them.  Every time (after at least 1 hour) of talking to them and getting them to agree it's $1000, I ask for an email or some kind of written documentation to say what they agreed on, they dodge this request and refuse to document this trade in value.  I've had at least 3 supervisors giving me their verbal "personal guarantee" that I will get my $1000 and 6 billing cycles later, I am still not getting it.  They really try to scam you in any way possible and hoping customers won't notice the little charges here and there, or hoping that we would just give up because no one has the tenacity of calling them over and over and spending tons of hours and mental stress dealing with them.

If I could give them a 0 I would. Worst company every. First they lied to us when we started a plan with them. We were told that we were getting a phone for free which was a complete lie. Then when we switch back to Verizon they continued charging us for a plan that was canceled. They owe us over $500. We keep getting the run around from costumer service. I’m reporting them to BBB. 

Oh, and in addition to their company being completely horrible their cell service sucks too.
I am came from Kansas City , with t-Mobil about 25 yrs,  I like the rate however the customer service are terrible . Keep me waiting . I am thinking about move to Verizon .
They overcharged me and charged me for items they told me I was getting for free then turned around and billed me for them. They are not are willing to help me

I have had the worst experience EVER joining T-Mobile about 6 months ago. It started out awful with the very first phone I got, then our bill was being raised every month without our knowledge then they charged us several fees for no reason. The reason I am giving them 5 stars is because today after 6 months of dealing with this nightmare I finally got Carrie Mary as a customer service rep who was able to fix my bill, lower it to an amazing $100 a month, she has been monitoring my bill for me so that this never happens again and I am FINALLY satisfied with my T-Mobile experience thanks to her. Without meeting her then I would have changed carriers that day. All other customer reps simply wanted to “look into it and give me a call back” which never happened.

I love T-mobile and has been with them for years never miss a payment but it’s beginning to be very messy they tell you one thing but do another they always say your call is being recorded but when you ask to hear it they don’t have the time to recover the conversation  they offered me FREE NEFLIX supose to have been within the household but they are charging me for a 2nd screen mind you one NEFLEX within the household but they are charging me and extra $6 for my bedroom tv $ 6 is not a lot but it adds up and takes away from my other bills I had already had NEFLEX and was happy then they say Oh you can get it FREE since you have T-mobile so automatically you will say YES but then every month you’re being charged I’ve called the last 3 months about the same issue and the 1st 2x they corrected the amount this time they actually found a way to explain and this is wat I get That’s a part of SCAMMING it’s FREE but you’re a paying $6 and yes $6 is a lot for a senior on a fixed income it’s jus pitiful the way they treat their customers after all these yrs it’s time to move on …..

I have been with t mobile for almost a year on on prepaid. The phone I bought from them has been giving me problems. I went there to get a new phone and contract. They gave me the ran around and wanted me to give them plus $400 dollars in deposit for a new phone.  I went to AT&T and got new phone 20min later . Now I want to downgrade my service on their prepaid number they are giving me the ran around again saying my account is suspended. I told them I don't have an account with them the number is prepaid. Hours on end no solution from the service desk. It's pathetic and I will never use this company again

Hello, after finding out the router I originally bought from T-Mobile needed an upgrade I thought that this would be an easy transition to a new upgraded one. Boy was I wrong this was a nightmare I wasted 2 weeks of back and forward with the store and technical support, and this was just a vicious circle of people not knowing what they were doing. Not sure if they were not configuring the router on purpose at the store because. Or again they just didn't know what they were doing. And this is after speaking with the manager and the rep. After the 3rd router, I was still unable to configure the router to log in. So I went back to the store to get a replacement the manager and rep started arguing with me because the court information wasn't on the router. Sand the manager stated after this router is replaced I cannot come back to this store if something goes wrong with the router. But it was their fault I had to go through so many routers before. So after replacing this router, I needed to make sure this router was up and running before I left the store while I was video on my phone this whole incident. This rep thought I didn't see him on his cell taking a screenshot of all the router information IMEI, router password, etc. So immediately after I left the store I called T-Mobile to tell them what had happened so this could be recorded. To me, the rep didn't sound too interested in this incident and was more concerned with a second line and another new phone. And now after putting in the "new router" overnight it reset itself and now I have had to reconfigure this router 3 times because it keeps getting hacked and I know it's because of the manager and rep at that store.  Wow... Store# 9062. I think they should retrain their management and staff because whatever they doing is not working. I'm in contact with a lawyer. This doesn't make any sense. I hope this doesn't happen to other customers at other stores like this happen to at this one.
I will be posting my experience with the store and Tmobile everywhere. This is crazy!!!

I have been a customer for at least 25 years. And have always been happy with them until yesterday. I went in to buy my granddaughter a phone which I raise. And noticed tax was way higher than it should of been, So I questioned it. They said its a one time activation fee of 35 added. I said since when! This is my phone number that I've had for years and we are switching the sim card over. And so the argument began. I will also mention I haven't worked in months because I've been busy fighting cancer. So I had to get in my bill money and take out the other money. This is nonsense. My husband and I both need new phones too. But they just lost two sales. I am going back to buying off of market place or Craigslist. Very very disappointed in this practice that started last year. And the lady waiting on me said it's always been that way! Wrong! I said I can't wait to get my survey when I leave the store. And for the first time ever they did not send me one.

I couldn't even set up an account online.  Something as simple as setting up an online account or account through the app became a problem.  I don't get it.  Their customer service stinks.  They transfer you to a call center out of the country and they can't resolve anything.

I was with Verizon for 22 years.When I saw the Ad. On tv about TMobil offer iPhone free for each line and their rates I called TMobil spoke to a sales representative (every thing was recorded by T Mobil) I told her I want to switch 5 lines from Verizon to T Mobil.She said we are eligeable for 5 iPhone 14 free.I told her I just want one iPhone 14Pro Max and one IPhone 14 Pro and I am not taking all iPhone 14 in that case TMobil saving lots of money but Insted 5 iPhone 14 free I want 1 iPhone 14 Pro Max free,and 1 iPhone 14 Pro free.I advised her to talk to her supervisor and if TMobil agrees to my deal I will switch.After 1/2 hours of explanation she agreed that I do not hand to pay any thing for iPhone 14 Pro Max and 7 $ a month for 24 months for I phone 14 pro.I agreed. Now T Mobil is keep charging money for both phone.I called T Mobil back many times but things are not redolved

Staff told us in person, we would not have any extra charges for our cruise trip to Bahamans, Jamaica, Haiti, with our Unlimited plan.  However, when we got a couple hundred $$$ bill they would not issue a credit to cover their employee's miss information. After speaking with C.S. and the manager James Soriano they opted to not honor their employee's word.  
This is rough as we are forced to use this company due to coverage for home use.  Its also painful to hear the staff and management ask us to call other people to fix this issue and all we got was another person passing the blame with no good results. 
Furthermore, each time I've walked in, staff was too busy to acknowledge me even if I was the only one in the store. Yesterday I had to watch Neisha walk around for 10 mins before she helped me and I felt like it was a problem that I was there. 
The issue here is their word is not been good with us now on 2 different occasions. 
It's not about the money here. Its about the lies and wasting other peoples time. Belmont NC store
